name: Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail
name_original: Punta Ballota y playa El Sable – circular desde Tagle
description: Jog a moderate 4.7-mile (7.6 km) circular route from Tagle, exploring Punta Ballota and the scenic El Sable and Santa Justa beaches.
region_intro: Jogging around Suances offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its dramatic Cantabrian coastline, expansive beaches, and lush green hills. The region features rugged cliffs, fine sandy shores, and the San Martín de la Arena Estuary, providing varied terrain for running. Coastal paths offer panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay, while inland routes explore the characteristic green hills of Cantabria. This blend of maritime and terrestrial features makes Suances a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Suances The most popular jogging route is Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes about 52 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic coastline around Punta Ballota and El Sable beach. Another top favourite among local runners is Playa de la Riberuca loop from Suances, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This route offers views of the estuary and connects to a pedestrian and cycling path. Local runners also love the Suances Sign Viewpoint – Playa La Tablia loop from Suances, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through coastal viewpoints and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Suances is defined by its diverse coastline, dramatic cliffs, and the San Martín de la Arena Estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed beach runs to more challenging cliffside paths. The routes in Suances are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Suances's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Suances?
A: Suances offers a good selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available. These range from moderate to more challenging options, providing diverse experiences for runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging paths in Suances?
A: The running paths in Suances feature a diverse landscape, including dramatic Cantabrian coastline, expansive sandy beaches like Los Locos and La Concha, and lush green hills. You'll find rugged cliffs, paths along the San Martín de la Arena Estuary, and scenic viewpoints offering panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Suances?
A: The running routes in Suances are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Many runners praise the varied terrain, the stunning coastal views, and the opportunity to explore both beaches and cliffside paths. Over 700 runners have used komoot to discover the area's trails.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Suances?
A: Yes, Suances features several excellent circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24723764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de la Riberuca loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8-mile (9.4 km) path offering estuary views. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14995460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suances Sign Viewpoint – Playa La Tablia loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.6 miles (12.3 km) through coastal viewpoints and beaches.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes in Suances?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are a couple of more challenging running routes available in Suances for those looking for a greater workout. These typically involve more significant elevation changes along the cliffs and hillsides.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Many running routes in Suances offer views of significant landmarks and natural attractions. You can pass by the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/781550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canallave Beach&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/640142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Quebrada Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic views from the Suances Lighthouse. The area also features beautiful beaches like Somocuevas and Santa Justa, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2589035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolao Cliff&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for families in Suances?
A: Suances offers paths that can be enjoyed by families, particularly those along the calmer stretches of La Concha Beach or the pedestrian and cycling paths near the San Martín de la Arena Estuary. These areas provide easier, flatter terrain for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Suances?
A: Many outdoor areas in Suances are dog-friendly, especially the beaches during off-peak seasons and the numerous coastal and inland trails. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, particularly in more populated areas or protected natural spaces.

Q: Are there running routes with good parking access in Suances?
A: Yes, many popular running routes in Suances, especially those near the beaches and the town center, offer convenient parking options. Routes starting from areas like Suances or Tagle often have accessible parking nearby, making it easy to begin your run.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Suances?
A: Suances is enjoyable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, ideal for exploring the coastal and inland trails. Summer is popular for beach runs, but it can be warmer, so early mornings or late evenings are best. Even winter can be suitable, with crisp air and dramatic coastal scenery, though some paths might be wet.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer spectacular viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The coastline around Suances is dotted with incredible viewpoints. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24709377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Suances Lighthouse – Playa de Los Locos loop from Tagle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24692551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Suances Lighthouse – Playa de Los Locos loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt; provide breathtaking panoramic views of the Cantabrian Sea, rugged cliffs, and the Suances Lighthouse itself.

Q: Are there any running routes that include beach sections?
A: Yes, several running routes in Suances incorporate beautiful beach sections. You can enjoy runs along the fine sands of Los Locos Beach or La Concha Beach. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999376317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores the scenic coastline around El Sable beach, offering a mix of cliffside paths and sandy stretches.
tips: Be careful approaching the cliffs on the right side. Vegetation covers the edge.
El Sable Beach, located in Tagle, a town in the municipality of Suances (Cantabria), is a natural gem perfect for those seeking tranquility and scenic beauty.

Also known as Ubiarco Beach. Nearby there is an old hermitage located in a cavity formed in the core of a spectacular anticline fold. It is a small, clean beach, with some picnic tables with a wonderful view. Recommended to go at any time of the year.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Punta Ballota and El Sable beach circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular jogging route is approximately 7.64 kilometers (4.75 miles) long. It typically takes about 51 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and any stops you make to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter about 4.16 km of hiking path, 1.39 km of road, 1.21 km of street, and 883 meters of other path types. Roughly 4.17 km are unpaved, 2.47 km are paved, and 920 meters are asphalt. Some sections, especially along the cliffs, may require careful footing, so appropriate footwear with good grip is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking coastal views throughout. Key highlights include the panoramic vistas from Punta Ballota, the tranquil beauty of El Sable Beach, and Santa Justa Beach, which features an old hermitage within a spectacular anticline fold. You'll also enjoy continuous views of the Cantabrian Sea and the rugged coastline.

faqs.question: Is the Punta Ballota and El Sable beach circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the coastal scenery with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially near cliffs or when encountering other people and wildlife. Check local regulations for specific leash requirements on beaches.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Punta Ballota and El Sable beach circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Tagle. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in the village of Tagle or near the access points to El Sable Beach. Look for designated parking areas or street parking that respects local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is during dry periods, ideally in spring, early summer, or early autumn when the weather is mild. It's advised not to use the trail when the ground is wet or during heavy rain and strong winds, as some sections can become slippery or hazardous, especially along the cliffs.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Punta Ballota and El Sable beach circular trail. It's a publicly accessible route along the beautiful Cantabrian coastline.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as 'moderate' for jogging. While much of it consists of accessible paths, some parts require good fitness and sure-footedness due to varied terrain and sections near cliffs. If you're a beginner, be prepared for some challenging sections and consider your fitness level.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tagle, the starting and ending point of the circular route, is a village where you can find local cafes and restaurants. Additionally, areas around El Sable Beach may offer options for refreshments or a meal after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: For this route, you should bring appropriate footwear with good grip due to the varied terrain. Also, carry water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a phone for emergencies. Consider bringing a light jacket, as coastal weather can change quickly. Binoculars can enhance your experience of the coastal views and potential wildlife.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: While specific public transport routes directly to the trailhead in Tagle aren't detailed, you would typically look for bus services connecting to Tagle from larger towns in Cantabria. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance, as services might be less frequent, especially outside peak season.
